issues.title: L1: month-turn engine (#612)
issues.body:
## L1 — Month-turn engine (#612)

Implements the ADR-0009 plan-months / two-speeds turn structure plus the workshop #3 spec addendum. The **month is the plan cadence**; the day-turn is demoted to a **resolution tick** beneath it (badge = calendar date; days-survived scoring stays fine-grained). Built as a **layer over the existing day-tick engine** rather than a re-grain — the sim substrate, RNG stream, and recorded replays are untouched, so this stays green and non-degrading.

### What landed (per SCOPE item)

1. **Month = plan turn, day = resolution tick.** `Clock` gains month helpers (`month_index` / `is_month_boundary` / `month_ordinal` / `month_label`). The HUD badge retires `Week 12 | … | Day 3/5` (which hung attention on the day tick, violating the guard rule) for the plan-month date badge. Also fixes the **latent leap-day infinite loop** (#620 note 2) that would have hung on Feb-29 dates across the 2017–2040 window. **Guard rule honoured: no routine decision hangs on the day tick.**
2. **Plan phase — Attention economy.** New `MonthPlan`: founder currency **Attention** (~20/month, `Balance.attention.per_month`, not hardcoded); explicit **reserve** that **evaporates crisply at month end** (no banking); **queued strategic actions with durations** (nothing strategic resolves instantly). Attention is introduced **alongside** the legacy AP pool (L2 owns deleting AP + migrating cost dicts — seam left clean, no big-bang). Staff actions remain per-person/separate.
3. **Event delivery tiers.** `EventTiers` classifies every genre **ambient / feed / window** with `source_id` provenance on feed items; only **windows** demand a decision. `WindowResolver` implements the costed menu **HANDLE-from-reserve / HANDLE-by-cannibalizing / DEFER (mints a Ledger entry, deferrable class only) / IGNORE (list price)**; unignorable flag supported; unanswered windows auto-resolve as IGNORE + a mild, data-driven rep penalty. Window **demand budget 2–3/month → 5–6 endgame** (Balance-driven) enforced in the controller.
4. **Day-tick playback + auto-pause-on-window.** `MonthController` advances ticks through `TurnManager`, routes events by tier, **pauses on windows**, resets the plan phase at month boundaries, and releases duration-elapsed WIP.
5. **Save/load survives all of it.** `MonthPlan` serialized into `GameState.to_dict/from_dict`; a **pause→save→load** smoke test proves an open window + reserve + WIP round-trip and playback resumes.
6. **Replay artifact.** `record_window_response` bumps the schema with `payment_source`; the **league (month) id** is stamped beside (seed, game_version). Format tag held at `v1` (additive; unknown log keys are ignored by the v1 simulator, so pre-L1 artifacts stay verifiable).

**Out of scope (left clean):** ADR-0015 doom-function migration is Lane 2 — this resolver/schema never reads/writes doom directly (routes through option effects + ledger factories only). The **full new plan *scene* UI** (explicit reserve dial, four-verb window menu incl. DEFER) remains attrition work — but the month loop is now **reachable from the playable scene** (see below).

### Playable month path (playtest follow-up)

Pip's first playtest hit the gap: the engine existed but End Turn still called `end_turn()` (single day-step) — zero L1 behavior reachable. Fixed (`e4cd408`):

- **End Turn / Commit Plan now call `GameManager.end_month()`**: commit queued actions as the month plan → **implicit reserve** = all unspent Attention (v1 default; the plan screen makes it a dial) → execute the open plan turn → **day-tick playback** (visible date advance, `day_tick_seconds` pacing).
- **Auto-pause-on-window**: windows present through the **existing `event_dialog`** (the L10 extraction built for exactly this), AP pre-stripped so displayed costs match what resolution charges. Choices route through `MonthController.resolve_current_window_option`: the event's ignore option → **IGNORE**; any other option → **HANDLE paid reserve-first, then by cannibalizing** — `payment_source` still lands in the replay artifact. (The explicit four-verb menu incl. DEFER is the plan screen's job.)
- **Month boundary → review dialog → next plan phase.** The boundary tick is **held open** as the new plan phase (`month_open_pending`) — never auto-executed, or its consequence steps would double-run when the plan commits. A plain month-review dialog closes into planning. Loop: **plan → play → review → plan**.
- **Old day-step is DEV-ONLY**: it survives solely as the DEV MODE overlay's "Day step (dev — old path)" button; `end_turn()` semantics unchanged (tests pin it). Guard rule holds: only windows pause the day ticks.
- Feed items surface as log lines with `source_id`; ambient stays silent.

### Honest build badge (playtest follow-up)

The baked `build_stamp.txt` (fd60eb6 · 2026-07-11) was stale on every branch and cost the playtest session (`a4e7661`). Dev checkouts now read **live git HEAD at runtime** (`git -C <repo-root> rev-parse`, gated on `.git` existing so exported builds never shell out; cached; worktree-safe): badge reads `DEV BUILD v0.11.0 · l1-month-turn-engine@a4e7661 · live`. Exported/no-git builds fall back to the baked stamp **explicitly marked `(stamp)`** — no badge form can pass as something it isn't, so two checkouts can never show the same badge silently. A stale stamp prints a console note.

### Local GUT results

CI's test gate is hollow (#629) — these are real local runs (Godot 4.5.1, after a headless `--import` pass):

- **Full `tests/unit/` run: 402/402 passing, 0 failures, 39 GUT scripts** (the 40th unit file, `test_verification_determinism.gd`, `extends Node` — a pre-existing non-GUT script — so no #590 hidden parse-failures).
- **New L1 suites (49 tests): all green.** `test_clock_month` (leap-day + month helpers), `test_month_plan` (Attention/reserve/durations/serialization), `test_event_tiers`, `test_window_resolver`, `test_month_controller` (demand budget, auto-pause, boundary hold-open, option→verb mapping, headless playable-loop smoke), `test_month_save_load` (pause→save→load), **`test_month_button_path`** (a headless smoke driving a FULL month through the exact End-Turn-button API: plan commit → ticks → auto-pause → review → next plan phase).
- **Touched regression suites green:** `test_game_manager` 35/35 (end_turn semantics pinned + unchanged), `test_replay_verification`, `test_save_load_roundtrip`, `test_game_state`, `test_events`, `test_engine_determinism`, `test_salary_cadence`, `test_liability_ledger`, `test_dev_build_indicator` (+2 badge-honesty tests).

### Schema decisions to eyeball

- **Attention alongside AP, not replacing it** — L1 introduces the new currency; L2 deletes the pool. Keeps blast radius sane.
- **Event schema additions:** `delivery_tier`, `event_class`, `source_id`, `unignorable`, `expiry_turns`, and a `window{ attention_cost, handle_option, ignore_option, defer{factory,amount} }` block. Un-annotated legacy popups default to **window** (behaviour-preserving).
- **Window options strip legacy `action_points` costs** — Attention is the window decision currency.
- **League id placeholder** = `YYYY-MM` of the run start month, pending the ADR-0016 pipeline.
- **`months_per_turn()` stays 1.0** (turn = workday) — the month is a layer, not a re-grain; this keeps risk/doom calibration and replays intact. Noted in-code for L2.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Add visual highlight effect for newly available actions
issues.body:
## Summary
When new actions become available (unlocked by meeting requirements), they should have a visual highlight effect until the player interacts with them.

## Proposed Implementation
- Track which actions player has 'seen' or interacted with
- New/unseen actions get a subtle glow or pulsing border
- Effect disappears after:
  - Player hovers over the action, OR
  - Player uses the action, OR
  - Next turn starts (optional)

## Example Use Cases
- Player publishes first paper → 'Submit Paper' action unlocks → highlighted
- Player reaches 30 reputation → 'Strategic' submenu unlocks → highlighted
- New hire types become available after certain turn → highlighted

## Technical Considerations
- Need to track 'seen' actions in GameState or separate tracking
- Could use shader effect or animated border
- Should be subtle, not distracting

## Priority
Nice-to-have - helps player discovery of new options

issues.title: Add hero art/images to event popup options
issues.body:
## Summary
Event popup buttons should have small 'hero art' images to make choices more visually appealing and help players quickly understand options.

## Proposed Implementation
- Add optional 'icon' field to event option definitions
- Display small art/icon next to each choice button
- Could use existing icon system or add event-specific art

## Example
For burnout event:
- Emergency Intervention: image of manager talking with team
- Team Retreat: image of outdoor bonding activity  
- Pizza Party: image of pizza boxes 🍕
- Push Through: image of stressed workers

## Priority
Nice-to-have, not blocking. Improves visual polish and player experience.

issues.title: Research Quality System (Rushed/Standard/Thorough)
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement research quality toggle that affects research speed, technical debt, and doom.

**Extracted from**: #429 (which covered Tech Debt + Upgrades + Research Quality)

---

## Feature Description

Three quality levels for research actions:

### Rushed
- **Speed**: 2x research generation
- **Cost**: Accumulates technical debt (+2 per turn)
- **Doom**: +1 doom per turn
- **Use Case**: Desperate situations, final push

### Standard
- **Speed**: 1x research generation (baseline)
- **Cost**: No debt
- **Doom**: Baseline doom
- **Use Case**: Normal gameplay

### Thorough
- **Speed**: 0.5x research generation
- **Cost**: No debt
- **Doom**: -1 doom per turn (safer!)
- **Use Case**: Safety-focused builds, early game

---

## Implementation

### Code Changes

```gdscript
# In actions.gd or new research_quality.gd:
const RESEARCH_QUALITY = {
    "rushed": {
        "research_multiplier": 2.0,
        "debt_per_turn": 2,
        "doom_modifier": 1.0
    },
    "standard": {
        "research_multiplier": 1.0,
        "debt_per_turn": 0,
        "doom_modifier": 0.0
    },
    "thorough": {
        "research_multiplier": 0.5,
        "debt_per_turn": 0,
        "doom_modifier": -1.0
    }
}
```

### Files to Modify
- `godot/scripts/core/actions.gd` - Add quality parameter to research actions
- `godot/scripts/core/game_state.gd` - Track current research quality setting
- `godot/scripts/core/turn_manager.gd` - Apply quality modifiers during turn processing
- `godot/scenes/main.tscn` or UI - Add quality selector (radio buttons/dropdown)

### Integration Points
- Technical Debt System (already implemented) - rushed research adds debt
- Doom System (already implemented) - quality affects doom modifiers

---

## UI Requirements
- Radio buttons or dropdown on research actions
- Show trade-offs clearly (speed vs safety vs debt)
- Default: Standard
- Remember last selection

---

## Estimated Effort
1-2 hours implementation + 1 hour testing

---

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Players can select research quality level
- [ ] Research speed scales with quality selection
- [ ] Rushed research accumulates technical debt
- [ ] Thorough research reduces doom
- [ ] UI clearly shows trade-offs

issues.title: Comprehensive upgrades system overhaul
issues.body:
## Summary
The 'upgrades' section needs a comprehensive overhaul tied into useful office and operational expenses.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Current upgrades feel disconnected from actual gameplay needs.

## Proposed Categories
### Office & Facilities
- Workspace quality (desks, chairs, lighting)
- Kitchen and amenities
- Meeting rooms
- Security systems
- Location upgrade (move to better office)

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- Compute clusters
- Development tools
- Network infrastructure
- Backup systems

### Software & Systems
- Accounting software (see #475)
- Project management tools
- Communication platforms
- HR systems

### Research Tools
- Datasets
- Specialized hardware
- Cloud credits
- Academic subscriptions

## Mechanics
- Each upgrade has:
  - Upfront cost
  - Ongoing maintenance
  - Tangible gameplay benefit
  - Clear prerequisite chain

## Integration
- Tied to budget management
- Visible in office representation
- Affects researcher productivity/morale
- Strategic choices: what to prioritize?

## Related Systems
- Office outfitting (#467)
- Finance management (#475)
- Personnel system

## Priority
High - core gameplay loop improvement

issues.title: Progressive finance and software management system
issues.body:
## Summary
Comprehensive finance management system where players upgrade from pen-and-paper to sophisticated software, with increasing complexity and automation options.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27 - extensive notes on finance system design.

## Core Concept
Players must manage increasing transaction volume and complexity. Better systems cost money but save time (AP). Creates interesting trade-off between efficiency and cost.

## Progression Tiers
### Tier 1: Manual (Free, High AP Cost)
- Pen and paper ledger
- Manual receipt tracking
- Scientists often forget receipts
- Manual payroll calculation
- High AP cost for basic accounting

### Tier 2: Spreadsheet ($100/year, Medium AP Cost)
- Excel/Sheets
- Formulas reduce errors
- Still manual data entry
- Better visibility
- Medium AP cost

### Tier 3: Basic Accounting ($1000/year, Low AP Cost)
- QuickBooks equivalent
- Automated categorization
- Invoice generation
- Still requires manual review
- Low AP cost

### Tier 4: Automated Payroll ($5000/year + per employee)
- Automatic payroll processing
- Tax calculations
- Direct deposit
- Minimal AP cost

### Tier 5: International Payroll ($15000/year + per employee)
- Multi-currency support
- International tax compliance
- Exchange rate handling
- Very low AP cost

### Tier 6: Enterprise ERP ($50000/year)
- Full automation
- Predictive budgeting
- Fraud detection
- Minimal AP cost, maximum functionality

## Required Initial Setup
Players must establish (or have in starting config):
- **Bank account** - where money lives
- **Email** - for communications
- **Tracking system** - how they know their balance (even if just mental math)

## Finance Actions & AP Costs
### Review Transactions (AP varies by system)
- Tier 1: 2 AP per 10 transactions
- Tier 6: 0 AP, auto-approved unless flagged

### Chase Missing Receipts (AP varies by culture)
- Some researchers forget receipts
- Requires follow-up
- Better culture = fewer missing receipts

### Approve Expenses (AP varies by system)
- Tier 1: Manual calculation and approval (high AP)
- Tier 6: Auto-approval with smart rules (0 AP)

### Payroll Processing (AP varies by system)
- Tier 1: Calculate hours, taxes, write checks (5 AP)
- Tier 4+: Automated (0 AP)

## Budget Management Features
### Project Budgets
- Researchers propose projects with budgets
  - Compute hours needed
  - Time estimate
  - Effort required
  - Travel for conferences
- Player approves/modifies budget
- Track spending vs budget
- Overspending warnings

### Recurring Expenses
- Rent, utilities, subscriptions
- Players must anticipate and plan
- Can doom yourself with commitments
- Example: Employee severance, lease agreements

### Commitments & Consequences
- Firing employees requires severance pay
- Breaking lease = penalty
- Subscription contracts
- Loan repayment schedules

## UI Enhancement Tie-In
Each software upgrade also enhances the game's UI:
- Tier 1: Basic text display
- Tier 3: Charts and graphs
- Tier 6: Beautiful dashboards, predictive analytics

## Complexity Scaling
As lab grows, transaction volume increases:
- 1-5 employees: ~50 transactions/month
- 5-20 employees: ~200 transactions/month  
- 20+ employees: ~500+ transactions/month

Without upgrades, this becomes unmanageable.

## Strategic Depth
### Trade-offs
- Hire admin staff OR buy better software?
- Cheap manual processes OR expensive automation?
- Focus budget on research OR infrastructure?

### Failure States
- Overdraft from missed payments
- Tax penalties from errors
- Researcher frustration from slow reimbursement
- Audit problems from poor records

## Implementation Phases
1. **Phase 1**: Basic transaction tracking and manual system
2. **Phase 2**: Software upgrade tiers 2-4
3. **Phase 3**: Project budgeting system
4. **Phase 4**: Advanced automation and ERP
5. **Phase 5**: UI enhancement tie-ins

## Related Systems
- Upgrades overhaul (#475)
- Project/research system (needs creation)
- Employee management
- Budget planning

## Design Principles
- Don't simulate actual accounting (boring)
- Represent complexity abstractly (transactions/month, AP cost)
- Make upgrades feel worth it
- Create genuine strategic choices
- Reward planning and investment in infrastructure

## Priority
High - core economic gameplay loop

## Notes
This is a major system that touches many parts of the game. Should be designed carefully and implemented incrementally.

issues.title: Automated Blog Publishing From Data Lake Events & Commits
issues.body:
This issue tracks design and implementation of an automated blog publishing system for the AI safety research game ecosystem, leveraging code and workflow elements from both pdoom-data and pdoom1 repositories.

**Goals:**
- Automate blog content creation from commit metadata, dataset refresh logs, delta event updates, and pipeline release notes (see docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md, scripts/blog_manager.sh in pdoom-data)
- Make blog generation repeatable, friendly to versioning, and tightly integrated with automated data pipelines
- Ensure each blog entry is traceable, with actionable links to relevant dataset commits, historical events, and game state delta updates
- Maintain world-best documentation and metadata for blog entries, with source attribution and validation, following contributing guidelines
- Automate publication to dev-blog/entries/ and DEVBLOG.md, compatible with CI and future Jira/GitHub automation
- Support cross-repo triggers, allowing event publication from both game (pdoom1) and data (pdoom-data) sides

**References to Implementation**:

- [scripts/blog_manager.sh](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/scripts/blog_manager.sh): Bash script for generating and publishing blog post files
- [DEVBLOG.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/DEVBLOG.md): Main developer blog document
- [docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md): Automated sync and publishing pipeline, PR automation, documentation standards
- [archive/session-docs/SESSION_SUMMARY_PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_2025-11-03.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/archive/session-docs/SESSION_SUMMARY_PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_2025-11-03.md): Data flow, zero manual intervention, world-best pipeline
- [docs/DATA_PUBLISHING_STRATEGY.md](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/docs/DATA_PUBLISHING_STRATEGY.md): Maintenance, update cadence, changelog management

---

**Relevant Code/Workflow Snippets:**

1. *Blog generation & publishing pipeline (pdoom-data)*:
   - [`scripts/blog_manager.sh` (function: publish_blog_post, post creation, validation, permanent release note storage)](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/scripts/blog_manager.sh#L73-L290)
   - [`DEVBLOG.md` and [`blog/`](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/tree/main/blog/) structure (timestamped blog files, development log aggregation)]
   - [Pipeline: commit → validation script → dev_metrics.db → DEVBLOG.md update]
   
2. *Sync pipeline from data repo to game repo (pdoom-data → pdoom1)*:
   - [Automated sync pipeline definition, validation, and transformation](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md#L10-L401)
   - [Sync script for game integration: `scripts/sync_to_pdoom1.sh`](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/scripts/sync_to_pdoom1.sh)
   - [GitHub Actions automation for data-to-game sync and test passes](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/docs/guides/PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md#L404-L435)
   
3. *Game event processing and blog traceability*:
   - [session summaries for integration architecture](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/blob/main/archive/session-docs/SESSION_SUMMARY_PDOOM_DATA_INTEGRATION_2025-11-03.md#L164-L194)
   - [Database schema for blog entries, cross-repo linkage, and analytics](https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom-data/blob/main/docs/INTEGRATION_PLAN.md#L118-L143)

---

**Action Items:**
- [ ] Design blog automation pipeline (event triggers, update hooks)
- [ ] Refactor code/scripts where needed to support reproducible publishing
- [ ] Ensure correct/complete metadata and provenance tracking for all published entries
- [ ] Implement and test automated publishing via CI/CD
- [ ] Document how to contribute content/events to the blog and trace its source

**Success criteria:**
- 100% blog posts generated with complete provenance and metadata
- Automated integration with game, website, and data pipelines
- Zero manual copy-paste required for publishing

---

**Expert Reference Code Snippets:**

*Blog post generation and publishing (pdoom-data):*
```bash
# In scripts/blog_manager.sh
create_blog_post() {
    # ...
    echo "Created blog post: $blog_file"
    echo "Edit the file to add your content, then run:"
    echo "  $0 publish $blog_file"
}
publish_blog_post() {
    local blog_file="$1"
    # ...
    echo "Blog file not found: $blog_file"
    exit 1
}
# ... Functionality for permanent, automated storage of release notes
```

*Automated sync pipeline for data → game integration (pdoom1):*
```bash
# Automated data sync from pdoom-data to pdoom1
PDOOM_DATA_REPO="$(pwd)"
PDOOM1_REPO="${1:-../pdoom1}"
# Validate, rsync data, track commits, test integration
mkdir -p "$PDOOM1_REPO/shared/data/historical_timeline"
rsync -av --delete "$PDOOM_DATA_REPO/transformed/timeline_events/" "$PDOOM1_REPO/shared/data/historical_timeline/"
```

*Blog database schema example (pdoom-data):*
```sql
CREATE TABLE blog_entries (
    entry_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    title VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    slug VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    metadata JSONB,
    published_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    source_repo VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'pdoom1',
    sync_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending'
);
```

*Session doc - summary of automation features:*
```
**Automated**: GitHub Actions trigger sync on pdoom-data commits
**Validated**: All data validated before sync
**Tested**: Integration tests run in pdoom1 PR
**Versioned**: Sync manifest tracks source commits
**Reversible**: Can rollback via git history
**Zero-sprawl**: Single source of truth (pdoom-data)
```

issues.title: Character Creation System (Point Allocation)
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement a character creation system where players allocate points to customize their starting position and abilities.

**Related to**: Risk System design (see `godot/docs/design/RISK_SYSTEM.md`)

---

## Feature Description

At game start, players can allocate points across:

### Starting Resources
- Extra money (runway buffer)
- Additional compute
- Starting reputation boost

### Starting Staff
- Pre-hired researchers (safety/capabilities mix)
- Starting manager

### Personality/Skills
- **Insight domains** (see #TBD - Insight System)
  - Political Insight (regulatory/public awareness visibility)
  - Financial Insight (funding/cash flow visibility)
  - Academic Insight (research integrity/capability overhang visibility)
  - Organizational Insight (insider threat visibility)
- Other personality traits TBD

### Default Class
- Pre-configured "default" option for players who don't want to customize
- Balanced starting position

---

## Design Notes

From design discussion (2025-12-25):
> "The game will have a 'build your character / starting equipment' section at the start where they can allocate points to give character eg extra money, staff, personality bonuses, etc, and default is just the default preconfig class."

This ties into:
- Insight system (skill-gated visibility into game mechanics)
- Upgrade rework (making upgrades more subtle)
- Player RPG element of the game

---

## UI Requirements
- Character creation screen before game start
- Point allocation interface
- Preview of starting position
- "Default" quick-start option
- Tooltip explanations for each option

---

## Dependencies
- Insight System (for skill allocation)
- Risk System (insight affects risk visibility)

---

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Character creation screen implemented
- [ ] Point allocation mechanic working
- [ ] At least 3 customizable dimensions
- [ ] Default class available
- [ ] Starting game state reflects allocations
- [ ] Integrates with insight system

issues.title: Character creation and lab setup point allocation system
issues.body:
## Summary
Add optional character creation where players spend points allocating advantages for lab setup (location, starting funds, reputation, connections, etc.).

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Give players agency over their starting conditions while keeping a default for those who want to jump in.

## Features
### Point Buy System
Players get X points to spend on:
- **Location**: Bay Area (expensive, talent pool) vs smaller cities (cheap, less talent)
- **Starting Capital**: More money = less points elsewhere
- **Reputation**: Start with academic credentials vs unknown
- **Network**: Have researcher friends to hire easily
- **Equipment**: Start with compute vs basic laptops
- **Institutional Support**: University affiliation, grants, etc.

### Default Option
- "Start Standard Game" button for players who don't want to optimize
- Balanced default setup

### Presets
- "Academic Spinoff" - university connections, low funds
- "Well-Funded Startup" - VC money, no reputation
- "Solo Researcher" - minimal everything, high autonomy

## Design Considerations
- Keep it simple (5-10 choices max)
- Make trade-offs meaningful
- Replayability: different builds = different experiences
- Balance: no "obviously best" choice

## Priority
Enhancement - adds replayability and player agency

issues.title: (Design) Create a schema to aallow for end game states based off Manifold market outcomes

milestone.title: Future Releases
milestone.description: Features and enhancements deferred beyond v1.0.0

issues.body:
There are prediction markets that we can programattically refer  to both the state of and changes to over time to influence pdoom meter

This probably falls under a larger heading of 'solve the pdoom mechanic for better complexity and depth'

issues.title: Office cosmetic shopping system
issues.body:
## Summary
Allow players to purchase small cosmetic items for their office by going shopping (plants, art, decorations, etc.).

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Add personality and attachment to the office space.

## Features
- Shopping action (takes time/AP)
- Categories: plants, art, office toys, coffee machines, etc.
- Small costs but morale/culture benefits
- Visual representation in office view
- Optional: staff preferences (some love plants, others don't care)

## Examples
- Potted plants (+morale, need maintenance)
- Coffee machine (productivity boost, ongoing cost)
- Foosball table (team building)
- Whiteboard walls (collaboration bonus)
- Art (culture/prestige)

## Game Impact
- Small AP/budget sink
- Morale and culture building
- Personalization and attachment
- "Treat yo self" moments

## Related Systems
- Office visual representation (#470)
- Office outfitting (#467)
- Budget management

## Priority
Enhancement - nice-to-have feature, low priority

issues.title: Visual office representation on screen
issues.body:
## Summary
Display the office environment visually on screen, showing layout, furniture, equipment, and staff presence.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Make the lab feel more tangible and alive.

## Features
- Top-down or isometric office view
- Show desks, equipment, common areas
- Staff presence indicators (who's in, who's traveling)
- Visual representation of upgrades (new equipment, better furniture)
- Could show activity: researchers collaborating, meetings, etc.

## Design Options
1. Static background with overlays
2. Simple 2D floor plan
3. Isometric view (StarCraft style)
4. Side-scrolling office (Game Dev Story style)

## Benefits
- Makes the lab feel real
- Visual feedback for upgrades
- Sense of growth and progress
- Attachment to the space and team

## Technical Considerations
- Asset requirements
- UI space allocation
- Performance impact

## Priority
Enhancement - significant UX improvement, requires art assets

issues.title: Employee living and office environment outfitting system
issues.body:
## Summary
Allow players to outfit the office and living environments of their staff, affecting morale, productivity, and retention.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Part of deeper personnel management and overhead cost system.

## Design Considerations
- Office quality affects productivity and recruitment
- Living environment affects retention and wellbeing
- Budget trade-offs between amenities and core research
- Could include: standing desks, ergonomic chairs, kitchen quality, gaming room, gym, etc.
- Living: housing assistance, quality of accommodation, location

## Related Systems
- Personnel morale/satisfaction
- Budget management
- Reputation with researchers

## Priority
Enhancement - adds depth to personnel management

issues.title: Extract 2018-2019 timeline from Alignment Research Dataset
issues.body: Extract historical AI safety/capabilities events for 2018-2019 from the Alignment Research Dataset and other sources. Target: 25-30 events for 2018, 30-35 for 2019. Requires pdoom-data repository first. 2017.json already created as proof of concept.

issues.title: Build lane L1 · Turn engine: plan-months, day ticks, response windows (ADR-0009)
issues.body:
**Authority:** ADR-0009 (+ ADR-0012 for window menu semantics). Work order: `docs/game-design/WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L1. **CRITICAL PATH** — L2/L3/L4/L5 queue behind this.

Scope:
- Month = the turn: plan phase (staff/founder-hour allocation, reserve, queued actions) → day-by-day resolution (speed control + auto-pause-on-window) → month review screen
- Guard rule: **no routine decision on the day tick**
- Response-window plumbing: costed menu [HANDLE-reserve / HANDLE-cannibalize / DEFER / IGNORE] (ledger side wired in L4)
- Reserve crisp (evaporates); delete banking
- Strategic actions get durations (fundraising money lands later)
- Re-denominate: salaries monthly, loans %/month placeholder, event pacing per month
- Replay schema bump: record (event_id, window, choice, payment_source); batch DQ-6 schedule provenance into same version
- Display badge = exact death date; score internals unchanged

Acceptance: deterministic replay round-trip incl. responses; constant-policy bots re-baselined (gate G1 prerequisite).

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read docs/game-design/WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md lane L1 and ADR-0009/ADR-0012. Also read the architecture-review findings comment on this issue before touching turn_manager. Implement L1; anything not covered by the ADRs goes to WORKSHOP_2_BACKLOG.md as a DQ, not into code."

⚠️ Wait for the arch-review comment (incoming) before starting.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
